components.notes: Audited `enkai check`, `enkai build`, and the cache-miss compile path for `enkai run` now prefer the self-host frontend/compiler path for supported programs. Full-language parser, resolver, typechecker, and codegen outside the audited self-host surface still ship through enkaic.

The shipped runtime is still not zero-Rust, but audited `enkai run` execution can now route supported programs through the self-host runtime core instead of executing user bytecode directly in enkairt. Scheduler, HTTP value model, and unsupported programs still fall back to enkairt.

The Rust CLI still owns command dispatch and most system surfaces. Audited `run`/`check`/`build`/`test` entrypoints now route through self-host compiler/runtime paths first where supported, but serve/model/deploy/worker/readiness/validation/release orchestration remain Rust-owned. The next peelable CLI/system tranches are isolated in `enkai/contracts/strict_selfhost_cli_system_slices_v3_2_0.json`, and readiness/validation-specific tranches are isolated in `enkai/contracts/strict_selfhost_readiness_validation_slices_v3_2_0.json`.
The shipped std/native path still depends on the Rust-owned FFI module and required native bindings.
Training/eval/serve runtime still depends on the Rust tensor backend and native acceleration stack.
Registry/model lifecycle behavior is implemented, but strict self-host policy still requires Rust-owned CLI/runtime layers and moves SQLite out of the release-blocking core.
